pattern: Missing aggregation in measure
Using ALL removes required filters
VALUES used where scalar expected
COUNT used on table instead of COUNTROWS
SUM used on non-numeric column
Incorrect filter context with CALCULATE and ALL
Missing relationship causing wrong results
fixed_dax: Create relationship between Sales and Category table

pattern: Using MAX instead of SELECTEDVALUE
Division without zero handling
DISTINCT used where scalar expected
Multiple filters overriding unintentionally
Incorrect use of EARLIER in measure
Using FILTER incorrectly for scalar comparison
Missing context transition in calculated column
Ambiguous many-to-many relationship
bad_dax: Model with many-to-many between Sales and Products
fixed_dax: Introduce bridge table to resolve many-to-many relationship

pattern: Incorrect time intelligence using manual filter
bad_dax: Sales YTD = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), FILTER(ALL(Date), Date[Date] <= TODAY()))

pattern: Using SUMX without row context need
Incorrect use of CALCULATE without filters
Using HASONEVALUE incorrectly
Incorrect logical comparison with BLANK
Incorrect OR condition using multiple filters
Using RELATED without relationship
fixed_dax: Create relationship between tables or use LOOKUPVALUE

pattern: Incorrect use of LOOKUPVALUE with multiple matches
fixed_dax: Ensure unique key or use aggregation like MAXX/FILTER

pattern: Incorrect use of ALLSELECTED for totals
Using SUMMARIZE incorrectly for aggregation
FILTER inside CALCULATE for simple equality
IF instead of DIVIDE
Using COUNTROWS(VALUES()) instead of DISTINCTCOUNT
Nested CALCULATE calls
Using FILTER on entire table unnecessarily
Redundant filters in CALCULATE
SELECTEDVALUE without default causes BLANK in card visuals
Time intelligence on fact date column instead of Date table
COALESCE not used to handle BLANK in chained measures
VAR not used when measure is referenced multiple times in SWITCH
fixed_dax:
Label =
VAR Rev = [Revenue]
RETURN SWITCH(TRUE(), Rev > 1000000, "High", Rev > 500000, "Medium", "Low")

pattern: ALL on Date table breaks time intelligence filter
ISBLANK used instead of checking denominator in DIVIDE
SUM of calculated column instead of measure
KEEPFILTERS missing when adding filter should intersect existing context
PARALLELPERIOD used instead of DATEADD for single-period offset
Inactive relationship not activated in measure
fixed_dax: Revenue by Ship Date = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), USERELATIONSHIP('Date'[Date], Sales[ShipDate]))

pattern: AVERAGE used on a ratio column instead of weighted average
TREATAS used with mismatched column data types
fixed_dax: Ensure both columns (Param[ID] and Fact[CustomerCode]) have the same data type before using TREATAS.

pattern: DATESBETWEEN with hardcoded dates instead of dynamic parameters
bad_dax: Q1 Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], DATESBETWEEN('Date'[Date], DATE(2024,1,1), DATE(2024,3,31)))

pattern: LASTDATE used as scalar date value
Fact date used in time-intelligence function
bad_dax: Rolling 30 Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), DATESINPERIOD(Sales[OrderDate], MAX(Sales[OrderDate]), -30, DAY))
fixed_dax: Rolling 30 Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), DATESINPERIOD('Date'[Date], MAX('Date'[Date]), -30, DAY))

pattern: Context transition inside FILTER predicate
bad_dax: High Revenue Products = CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(Product, [Revenue] > CALCULATE(AVERAGE(Sales[Amount]))))
fixed_dax: High Revenue Products = VAR AvgAmount = AVERAGE(Sales[Amount]) RETURN CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(Product, [Revenue] > AvgAmount))

pattern: Measure evaluated repeatedly inside SWITCH branches
fixed_dax: Segment = VAR RevenueValue = [Revenue] RETURN SWITCH(TRUE(), RevenueValue > 1000000, "High", RevenueValue > 500000, "Medium", RevenueValue > 0, "Low", "None")

pattern: Bi-directional virtual relationship using INTERSECT
Virtual relationship using CONTAINS
bad_dax: Customer Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(Sales, CONTAINS(VALUES(Customer[CustomerID]), Customer[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID])))

pattern: Overuse of ALLEXCEPT on wide fact table
SUMMARIZE used before TOPN ranking
bad_dax: Top Product Sales = SUMX(TOPN(10, SUMMARIZE(Sales, Product[ProductName], "Sales", [Revenue]), [Sales], DESC), [Sales])
fixed_dax: Top Product Sales = SUMX(TOPN(10, ALL(Product[ProductName]), [Revenue], DESC), [Revenue])

pattern: GROUPBY with CURRENTGROUP for simple aggregation
bad_dax: Grouped Sales = SUMX(GROUPBY(Sales, Sales[Region], "Amt", SUMX(CURRENTGROUP(), Sales[Amount])), [Amt])

pattern: ALL used inside RANKX scope without limiting dimension
Dynamic segmentation using disconnected table without range pruning
fixed_dax: Segment Customers = VAR MinValue = MIN(Segment[Min]) VAR MaxValue = MAX(Segment[Max]) RETURN COUNTROWS(FILTER(VALUES(Customer[CustomerID]), [Revenue] >= MinValue && [Revenue] < MaxValue))

pattern: ISINSCOPE logic missing cached base measure
USERELATIONSHIP inside iterator
Role-playing date logic duplicated instead of base measure
bad_dax: Ship Sales YoY = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), USERELATIONSHIP(Sales[ShipDate], 'Date'[Date]), SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Date'[Date]))

pattern: CALCULATETABLE used where CALCULATE scalar is sufficient
EXCEPT used for anti-join against fact table
NATURALINNERJOIN used in measure
NATURALLEFTOUTERJOIN used in measure
PATH functions used in high-cardinality measure
bad_dax: Org Depth Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(Employee, PATHCONTAINS(Employee[Path], SELECTEDVALUE(Employee[EmployeeID]))))
fixed_dax: Org Depth Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], EmployeeBridge[AncestorEmployeeID] IN VALUES(Employee[EmployeeID]))

pattern: GENERATESERIES created inside measure
UNION of filtered fact tables inside measure
bad_dax: Open Or Closed Sales = SUMX(UNION(FILTER(Sales, Sales[Status] = "Open"), FILTER(Sales, Sales[Status] = "Closed")), Sales[Amount])
fixed_dax: Open Or Closed Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[Status] IN {"Open", "Closed"})

pattern: INTERSECT of fact-key sets inside measure
Measure uses bidirectional CROSSFILTER override
bad_dax: Bi Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], CROSSFILTER(Customer[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID], BOTH))

pattern: REMOVEFILTERS applied to entire model table unnecessarily
SUMX over filtered fact table with direct additive column
SELECTCOLUMNS projection used before simple count
MAXX over fact table for scalar date instead of MAX
SUMX with FILTER over large fact for simple filtered sum
AVERAGEX over fact table for simple average of a column
COUNTROWS with FILTER for simple threshold count
Cumulative total using FILTER(ALL(Date)) instead of DATESYTD
RANKX missing Dense tie-handling causes rank gaps
TOPN missing sort direction defaults to ascending
HASONEVALUE used for hierarchy level detection instead of ISINSCOPE
SWITCH VAR missing — measure recalculated per branch
bad_dax: Period Metric = SWITCH([Sel], 1, [Revenue YTD], 2, [Revenue MTD], 3, [Revenue QTD], [Revenue])
fixed_dax:
Period Metric =
VAR Sel = [Sel]
RETURN SWITCH(Sel, 1, [Revenue YTD], 2, [Revenue MTD], 3, [Revenue QTD], [Revenue])

pattern: DISTINCTCOUNT on text name column instead of integer key
VAR not used when complex sub-expression appears in multiple branches
bad_dax: Margin Check = IF(DIVIDE(SUM(Sales[Profit]), SUM(Sales[Revenue])) > 0.3, "Good", IF(DIVIDE(SUM(Sales[Profit]), SUM(Sales[Revenue])) > 0.1, "OK", "Poor"))
fixed_dax:
Margin Check =
VAR Margin = DIVIDE(SUM(Sales[Profit]), SUM(Sales[Revenue]))
RETURN IF(Margin > 0.3, "Good", IF(Margin > 0.1, "OK", "Poor"))

pattern: Missing relationship causing wrong results
fixed_dax: Create relationship between Sales and Category table

pattern: Missing context transition in calculated column
Ambiguous many-to-many relationship
bad_dax: Model with many-to-many between Sales and Products
fixed_dax: Introduce bridge table to resolve many-to-many relationship

pattern: Using RELATED without relationship
fixed_dax: Create relationship between tables or use LOOKUPVALUE

pattern: Using SUMMARIZE incorrectly for aggregation
Fact date used in time-intelligence function
bad_dax: Rolling 30 Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), DATESINPERIOD(Sales[OrderDate], MAX(Sales[OrderDate]), -30, DAY))
fixed_dax: Rolling 30 Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), DATESINPERIOD('Date'[Date], MAX('Date'[Date]), -30, DAY))

pattern: Bi-directional virtual relationship using INTERSECT
Virtual relationship using CONTAINS
bad_dax: Customer Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(Sales, CONTAINS(VALUES(Customer[CustomerID]), Customer[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID])))

pattern: NATURALINNERJOIN used in measure
NATURALLEFTOUTERJOIN used in measure
PATH functions used in high-cardinality measure
bad_dax: Org Depth Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(Employee, PATHCONTAINS(Employee[Path], SELECTEDVALUE(Employee[EmployeeID]))))
fixed_dax: Org Depth Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], EmployeeBridge[AncestorEmployeeID] IN VALUES(Employee[EmployeeID]))

pattern: GENERATESERIES created inside measure
INTERSECT of fact-key sets inside measure
Measure uses bidirectional CROSSFILTER override
bad_dax: Bi Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], CROSSFILTER(Customer[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID], BOTH))

pattern: Flat denormalized table used instead of star schema
bad_dax: Single wide table with CustomerName, ProductName, RegionName, Date, Amount all in one table
fixed_dax: Split into: FactSales (keys + Amount), DimCustomer, DimProduct, DimRegion, DimDate. Create relationships on surrogate keys.

pattern: No dedicated Date table — using date column from fact table
fixed_dax: Create a dedicated Date table with continuous dates, mark it as Date table. Use DATESYTD('Date'[Date]) instead.

pattern: Calculated columns used for business logic instead of measures
fixed_dax: Margin % = DIVIDE([Total Revenue], [Total Cost]) — use as a measure so filter context applies correctly.

pattern: Many-to-many without bridge table causes incorrect totals
bad_dax: Many-to-many relationship set between Sales[CustomerID] and Budget[CustomerID]
fixed_dax: Create a Customer bridge table with unique CustomerID. Relate Sales → Customer ← Budget through the bridge.

pattern: Role-playing date dimension not using USERELATIONSHIP
Technical surrogate keys visible in report field list
bad_dax: CustomerKey, ProductKey, DateKey visible in field pane and draggable into visuals
fixed_dax: Right-click each key column in Model view > Hide. Users should never need to drag raw keys into visuals.

pattern: Measures not organized into display folders
bad_dax: All 40 measures in a flat unsorted list in the field pane
fixed_dax: Group measures into display folders: Revenue, Cost & Margin, Time Intelligence, KPIs, Operational. Set in Model view or Tabular Editor.

pattern: Bidirectional relationship between fact tables causes ambiguity
bad_dax: Bidirectional filter between FactSales and FactBudget
fixed_dax: Remove the bidirectional filter. Use CROSSFILTER or TREATAS inside specific measures that need cross-fact filtering.

pattern: Incremental refresh not set on large fact table
bad_dax: Full table refresh on 100M+ row fact table on every scheduled refresh
fixed_dax: Configure incremental refresh: add RangeStart/RangeEnd parameters in Power Query, set policy to store N years and refresh last N days.

pattern: Composite model DirectQuery fact table with no aggregation table
bad_dax: All visuals query DirectQuery fact table with no Import-mode aggregation layer
fixed_dax: Add an aggregation table (Import mode) summarized at day/month + key dimension granularity. Map it as user-defined aggregation in Manage Aggregations.

pattern: Snowflake schema with multi-hop dimension relationships
bad_dax: Sales → Product → Category → Division — three hops from fact to top-level dimension
fixed_dax: Flatten the dimension hierarchy into one DimProduct table with Category and Division columns. Reduces join complexity and improves query performance.

pattern: Currency conversion using SUMX without exchange rate date matching
fixed_dax: USD Revenue = SUMX(Sales, Sales[Amount] * LOOKUPVALUE(ExchangeRate[Rate], ExchangeRate[Date], Sales[OrderDate], ExchangeRate[Currency], Sales[Currency]))
